assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item denominated in a currency different from the functional currency of at least one group entity.

The stated foreign exchange exposure in the consolidated financial statements means the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item that is not fully eliminated on consolidation to be designated as a hedged item, and a recognised receivable is a candidate for fair value hedge accounting.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately allows cash flow hedge accounting for variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability, so the same eligible intragroup monetary-item exposure can also be considered under the cash flow hedge model.
